name: Raktabīja

distinctive_features: Multiple identical clones arising from blood drops
Wields traditional weapons like swords and clubs
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth skin with a sheen of blood

domain_specific_visual.WEAPONRY: Carries swords, clubs, and other traditional weapons
domain_specific_visual.CLONING_FEATURE: Each drop of blood creates a clone

domain_specific_functional.MYTHOLOGICAL_ROLE: Represents the challenge of overcoming seemingly insurmountable evil

relationships.description: Fought against Durga and her forms in battle
Battled with Kali who consumed his blood to prevent cloning

contextual_attributes.origin: Hindu mythology, specifically from the Puranas
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the regenerative nature of evil and the power of divine intervention

contrastive_constraints: NOT without the ability to clone from blood drops
